name: AmpSysCadencePlugin
notes: GUI/SKILL/CLI wrappers are readable; standalone GUI executables are bundled for open-box use.
Private AmpSys engine packages are compiled or frozen into standalone native core binaries.
Cadence SKILL prefers AMPSYS_GUI_EXE or bundled gui/<platform>/ampsys_gui before falling back to py -3.
The public runner selects the core binary by OS/CPU, not by the user's Python minor version.
Optimization progress and metric plots come from telemetry, so verbose output can stay off.
Public GUI/log/result telemetry shows convergence instead of raw fitness values.
The convergence trace now uses public best-score improvement, not generation count.
Population metrics are shown as a radar/spider scatter chart.
Balanced objective weights are adjustable in GUI Settings: GBW, Gain, Power, Noise, CMRR, PSRR, Area.
Differential-only metrics such as CMRR/PSRR are shown only when the engine reports them; area is reported when sizing data exists.
Windows core is an onedir executable at core/windows_amd64/ampsys_core/ampsys_core.exe.
Linux core is distributed as core/linux_x86_64.tar.gz and extracted by install_linux.sh.
For stronger commercial anti-tamper, run a VMProtect/Themida-style protector with --protector-cmd.
